message:
fix(session_index): replace unwrap_or(i64::MAX) with explicit overflow error for SQLite integer casts

The u64→i64 conversions for message_count and size_bytes in
SessionIndex::upsert_meta and rebuild_from_scratch previously used
unwrap_or(i64::MAX), silently clamping values that exceeded i64 range.
This masked data corruption: a session with message_count > i64::MAX
would be indexed with an incorrect count, and queries would return
wrong results without any error signal.

Introduces sqlite_i64_from_u64() helper that returns a typed
Error::Session when the value exceeds SQLite INTEGER range, failing
the indexing operation early with a clear diagnostic message.

Also adds:
- Unit test index_session_snapshot_rejects_message_count_over_i64_max
  that verifies out-of-range values produce the expected error
- Updated proptest strategy to branch on i64::MAX boundary, asserting
  that overflow values error and in-range values round-trip correctly

test(rpc): add backpressure edge-case tests for try_send_line_with_backpressure

Three new unit tests covering scenarios not previously exercised:
- Disconnect detection while channel is full (receiver drops mid-wait)
- High-volume ordering preservation under sustained backpressure
  (256 lines through a 4-slot channel, verifying count and order)
- Partial JSON line preservation without trailing newline

These close coverage gaps around the channel backpressure retry loop
that were identified during cross-surface parity analysis.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
